# Waiver: `<requirement being waived>`

<!-- Skeleton for a waiver. A waiver is a decision not to meet a requirement,
     recorded so it stays a decision instead of decaying into an oversight.
     Copy the filled table into the "Recorded waivers" section of
     CONTRIBUTING.md; this file is the shape, not the register.
     Placeholders are written as `<...>`; replace them, backticks and all. -->

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Requirement | `<the requirement, named precisely enough to check>` |
| Scope | `<what it applies to: whole repository, one stack, one workflow>` |
| Reason | `<why meeting it is wrong or impossible here>` — not "no time". A waiver records a considered decision; cost alone is a backlog item. |
| Owner | `<who is accountable while the waiver stands>` |
| Approver | `<who accepted the risk>` — not the owner, unless the repository has a single maintainer and that fact is itself recorded. |
| Revisit trigger | `<the condition that reopens this>` — stated so anyone can tell whether it has happened. Prefer an observable event over a date. |
| Tracking issue | `<link>`, or `None` plus the reason — for example, issues are disabled on this repository. |

## Notes

<!-- Optional. What was considered and rejected; what would change the answer. -->

Anything a future reader needs that the table cannot hold.
